summarize.prompt:
Summarize this in 3-5 concise bullet points. Lead with the most important:

{text}

action-items.prompt:
Extract every actionable item from this text. Number them. Be specific — include who, what, when if present.

{text}

explain.prompt:
Explain this in plain language. No jargon. Short paragraphs. Assume I'm smart but unfamiliar.

{text}

prescriber-explain.prompt:
Rewrite this as a clear, concise clinical note I could show my NP at an appointment. Professional but readable. Focus on what's clinically relevant. Remove filler.

{text}

parts-search.description: Format parts list for Canadian supplier search
parts-search.prompt:
Extract all components and parts from this text. For each: quantity, value/spec, type. Then suggest the best search query for Digi-Key Canada (digikey.ca) or Mouser Canada. I'm in Regina, SK.

{text}

obd-explain.description: Explain OBD data in plain English
obd-explain.prompt:
Explain this OBD data in plain English for a non-mechanic. What do the fuel trim values mean? Any concerning patterns? What should I physically check next? My truck is a 2007 Honda Ridgeline J35A9 (MAP-only, no MAF). Bank 2 = front/radiator side.

{text}

med-check.description: Check medication interaction against Jacob's stack
med-check.prompt:
Check this medication/supplement for interactions with Jacob's current stack: Vyvanse 50mg, Venlafaxine (SNRI), Trazodone 100mg. Flag anything dangerous. Note if it's available in Canada and if Saskatchewan Drug Plan covers it. Be direct — no excessive disclaimers.

{text}

journal-prompt.prompt:
Based on this context, give Jacob one specific journaling prompt. It should be grounded, recovery-aware, and not preachy. One question only. Something that opens a door rather than demanding an answer.

{text}

chatbox-brainstorm.description: Format a topic for multi-LLM chatbox brainstorming
chatbox-brainstorm.prompt:
Prepare this topic for a multi-LLM brainstorming session. Structure it to encourage diverse perspectives and creative solutions:

{text}
